subject: Project Management
title: Implementation Phase
description: A tiny lesson with a big payoff: Implementation Phase.

hook.text: Implementation is where developers finally write code — often months after the project started.

buildup.text: Developers follow the design specification, translating diagrams into working code module by module.

discovery.text: In waterfall, coding is supposed to be the 'easy part' — just follow the blueprint. Reality disagrees.

twist.text: Developers discover design flaws, missing requirements, and impossible assumptions — but the plan says 'no going back.'

climax.text: Workarounds accumulate. Code quality suffers. The implementation matches the spec but not the actual need.

punchline.text: Following a flawed plan perfectly still produces a flawed product.

quiz.question: What commonly happens during the waterfall implementation phase?
options: Developers discover design flaws but are discouraged from going back to fix them
The code perfectly matches all requirements
No issues arise because the design was thorough
